1989_10_06-India-Mumbai-Krishna_Consciousness_for_Everyone-OT_st2-vr01

Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

Meta Data v3

Event Name

Krishna Consciousness for Everyone

Event Date

06 Oct 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai, Mahaprabhu Prabhu’s house

Event Type

Private

Transcriber

Govinda Sundara das

Completed

31 Aug 2021

Source Audio

http://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-064-Krishna%20Consciousness%20For%20Everyone%20(Mumbai)-OT-dt_89_10_06.mp3

Source Video

NA

Verse

SB 11th Canto

Places

Varanasi

Pastimes

King Nimi’s respect for the Nine Yogendras, Lord Caitanya delivering the Mayavadi Sanyasis

Persons

Lord Rishabh Dev, King Nimi, Nine Yogendras, Lord Caitanya, Prakasananda Saraswati

Themes

The Ultimate goal of life, essence of all scriptures, glories of the Holy name, importance of spiritual education in rulers

Lecture Transcription

[Prayers]

Ideal kings

In the eleventh canto of Srimad-Bhagavatam, the sons of Lord Rishabh Dev - the nine Yogendras had met with the great Maharaja Nimi. Maharaj Nimi at the time was king. He lived in a palatial palace and was served by countless obedient and faithful servants.

Today political leaders, when they come into the position of power, sometimes they make a show of meeting with the poor, the forsaken and the ordinary people just as the methods to get the sympathy of the public so the people will vote for them. But very seldom do they really have a genuine concern for the needs of the people as much as their concern is to maintain their own prestige and their own position of power. This is the state of politics throughout the world and therefore wherever you go in the world it's practically rare to find anyone who likes the politicians of their country. In America I know of course most thoroughly because that is my homeland. And this particular election which took place last November, I never met anybody that either that liked at all either candidate. The whole of America was disgusted and disappointed that this is the best we have. We are supposed to be the greatest, the mightiest, the most intelligent advanced country in the world and these are the two men who are supposed to be the leaders, the emblems, the symbols of America and this is the best we could come up with is these two.. What’s going on? They were in this predicament...even the Americans would have to agree that they were living in Kali Yuga.

So it was really what everyone was asking was who's gonna make less of a mess huh... this was the poem that was going around... who will make less mess and when the two candidates were debating , really neither of them showed any real qualifications. All they were trying to do was to prove that if the other one was in office he would create a bigger mess than if I am in office. Really... literally if you watch the videos or hear the dialogue of their debates they were simply accusing what kind of a mess you would put the country if you were in, I wouldn't do like that and everyone watching what they were thinking - now the question is the person who wins this debate is the person who convinces me that the other is going to make a worse mess than he will. So therefore I'll vote for him. And nobody believes anything anyone says anymore...Hah... in politics who believes anything anyone says anymore. Everyone is simply thinking they're just saying this for votes, they'll say anything for votes. What they mean what they feel God knows.

But you see Maharaj Nimi he was not like that... he was a genuinely great King… Why? He was trusted by all his subjects for the simple reason that he was humble before the saintly persons. This in fact was the first and most important qualification of a king in civilized Vedic society, that the King had to be the humble servant of the sadhus and that he ruled his kingdom according to their direction not according to his whims or according to the advice of his mundane ministers but according to the advice of those great self-realized souls who truly had the spiritual interest of all living beings in heart.

So, Maharaj Nimi while sitting on his throne was one time visited by the nine Yogendras - the sons of Maharaja Rishabha Dev. As we know Maharaja Rishabha Dev was the king of earth. And being a true example to all of his subjects, when he attained an age where his children can take over the kingdom, he handed over his kingdom to Maharaja Bharata of whom the land of India was named after Bharat Varsha. But before handing the kingdom over to his son, he instructed his hundred sons in the science of self-realization. His first instruction -

nāyaṁ deho deha-bhājāṁ nṛloke
kaṣṭān kāmān arhate viḍ-bhujāṁ ye
tapo divyaṁ putrakā yena sattvaṁ
śuddhyed yasmād brahma-saukhyaṁ tv anantam

[SB 5.5.1]

[Lord Ṛṣabhadeva told His sons: My dear boys, of all the living entities who have accepted material bodies in this world, one who has been awarded this human form should not work hard day and night simply for sense gratification, which is available even for dogs and hogs that eat stool. One should engage in penance and austerity to attain the divine position of devotional service. By such activity, one’s heart is purified, and when one attains this position, he attains eternal, blissful life, which is transcendental to material happiness and which continues forever.]

That now my sons… that you have attained this very rare and valuable human life do not waste like the dogs and hogs that eat stool. They are wasting their life simply seeking out sense gratification. The process of sense gratification is meant for those species of life. It is not meant for a human being. tapo divyaṁ - human life is meant to accept tapasya for the divine realization of Devotion to God. By performing sacrifice for the purpose of Devotion to God - śuddhyed yasmād brahma-saukhyaṁ tv anantam - one attains the ultimate peace and happiness, the bliss of the soul. That is the true fulfilment of all our desires and all our goals in life. These teachings of Lord Rishabh Dev to his hundred sons contain in essence - all knowledge of how anyone in any walk of life can live within this world and attain the ultimate goal of self-realization.

Importance of spiritual knowledge in rulers

Now please understand who Lord Rishabh Dev was speaking to. He was speaking the system of Jnana, Vairagya and bhakti. How within the process of bhakti is all knowledge and all renunciation. Now of his hundred sons one of them Bharat Maharaj became the king of the earth and in the footsteps of his father he was the most responsible, most sensitive, most caring king for all of his subjects. The reason why the land of India was named Bharat Varsha is because this Maharaja Bharata was such a loving King that each member of his kingdom of all the millions and millions and millions of people on earth he treated everyone as a father loves and treats his own son. Not only human beings but even the most insignificant creatures. He would give his life to protect the welfare of all living things. He guided everyone according to the highest principles of morality, ethics and spiritual principle and anyone who deviated, he was very very strict and therefore practically no one dared to deviate and, in this way, there was no shortage of anything. There was complete prosperity in all four directions. Now amongst those hundred sons, there were also the nine Yogendras. Their only business was to simply wander throughout the world enlightening everyone in the science of devotion. They had absolutely no cares for anything of this world. They never even wore clothes. They roamed naked not caring about eating or sleeping, not caring about any fixed residence... with only one concern in their mind - to enlighten everyone in the science of devotion to Krishna.

So, see how these same instructions of Lord Rishabh Dev influenced the lives of the most responsible and honourable kings and at the same time the most renounced, aloof sadhus. You see there is a need for both. There is a need for renounced sadhus within society, there is a need for kings, politicians, there is a need for businessmen, industrialists, doctors, lawyers, agriculturalists, farmers.

Importance of spiritual teachers

Sometimes I am challenged, especially at universities. Recently I was at an accounting school where there was about a hundred students training to be Chartered accountants and one of them stood up and said, “This Krishna Consciousness that you're talking about is totally impractical for the society today. What would possibly happen to this world if everyone became a sadhu like you, who would take care of the, of the important functions of providing, who would regenerate the population?” I told them as far as regenerating the population; please don't worry about that problem. The problem is quite the opposite - who is willing to stop regenerating the population. But he said, “But it's not practical if everyone becomes a sadhu like you, so what is the use of this movement.” So, I asked him- what happens if everyone becomes an accountant like you huh… what happens if everyone on earth becomes an accountant like you… who's gonna who's gonna grow the food? Who’s gonna do the banking… not only that but all the accountants would be unemployed because there would be nobody to keep their accounts for... huh... the whole earth would be unemployed with all kinds of laid off accountants. So, the fact is there is a need for every class of men and the word of God is for everyone.

So, when Lord Rishabh Dev spoke, he spoke one divine message of how to perfect a human form of life and that same message was applied to King Bharat who became the first-class most important King in all history... and the same words were applied to the life of the nine Yogendras and they became the most celebrated sadhus.

Krishna says in Gita – cātur-varṇyaṁ mayā sṛṣṭaṁ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśaḥ [BG 4.13]. That, he created the varnashrama system, he created the four spiritual and social classes because there is a need for each and every one of them in order for the society to progress forward toward the proper destination but each one according to his natural inclinations and propensities in life must live according to the one supreme truth, the goal of life which is devotion to God. If you live according to the will of God aspiring for that one goal of bhakti devotion to God, then whatever classification you may follow within society you are perfect.

Humility of Maharaj Nimi

When the nine Yogendras came to the kingdom of Maharaj Nimi, Maharaj Nimi displayed the most important and essential quality of a leader - his ability to humble himself from the core of his heart before the great saints. Can you imagine if nine naked men with long matted hair came to the White House…Han.. What President Bush would do…he would say who are these hippies.. they are coming to demonstrate in front of the White House and he would immediately have the CIA, the CID, the Secret Service and FBI come and arrest them. And the least thing they'd be concerned is hearing philosophy from them...han. But when Maharaj Nimi saw these very very renounced Sadhus coming, he was so happy. Tears were bursting from his eyes, his hairs were standing on end, he was thinking such a fortune these great souls have come to bless me. He got off of his throne and washed their feet with his own hands and sprinkled out water on his head and on the heads of all of his family members for the purification of their lives and then with a humble heart full of sincere desire he inquired from them as to what is the goal of life and how to attain it. Of course he was a great saint himself. Spiritually he was equal to the nine Yogendras. But, in order to show the proper example of how a king must receive great saints he humbled himself and all the problems that all living beings must face on the spiritual path, he presented those problems to the nine Yogendras so that their sacred words would eternally be the guide for all of us.

During the course of the discussion, one of these great Souls was explaining the various incarnations of the Lord in the in the four Yugas. How the Lord appears in Satya Yuga, how he appears in treta yuga and dwapara yuga, for what purpose he appears and the Yuga Dharma-the particular type of spiritual discipline that is required for that particular age and that great verse which prophesized the divine descent of Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu was spoken at that time to Maharaj Nimi –

kṛṣṇa-varṇaṁ tviṣākṛṣṇaṁ

sāṅgopāṅgāstra-pārṣadam

yajṣaiḥ saṅkīrtana-prāyair

yajanti hi su-medhasaḥ

[SB 11.5.32]

[In the Age of Kali, intelligent persons perform congregational chanting to worship the incarnation of Godhead who constantly sings the names of Kṛṣṇa. Although His complexion is not blackish, He is Kṛṣṇa Himself. He is accompanied by His associates, servants, weapons and confidential companions.]

‘In the age of kali Yuga, those who have good intelligence, they will worship that beautiful form of Krishna who does not come in a blackish form. And he comes in a beautiful golden form and he comes with his eternal associates to perform the Sankirtana movement - the congregational chanting of the holy names and those with real good intelligence they will worship that beautiful form of God by the process of congregationally chanting the holy names of the Lord.’

Glories of chanting the Holy names

So, this evening, many guests they may be wondering what are these people doing in the middle of the night dancing around and singing like this…Han…it's not to... After all, usually if you dance the man chooses a nice girl and they dance together… huh... in the west this is what they asked when they see us dancing in the street… what is this nonsense why all the men are on one side and all the women on the other side. This is not the way we dance in this country. Dancing is meant to enjoy.. han.. the concept of dancing in the western world is the boy and the girl enjoy the closeness of each other's bodies as they are dancing together and this is considered great fun. So what fun is there if all the women are on one side and all the men are in the other.. han.. Because we are not dancing for our personal enjoyment. We are dancing for the pleasure of God. We are dancing to glorify God and therefore there must be no trace of this materialistic sensual motivation.

In the age of Kali Yuga there is no more powerful and effective form of meditation than chanting the holy name and dancing. This is the verdict of the Shastra and this is the verdict of the great acharyas. The nine Yogendras predicted four thousand five hundred years before the descent of Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, at least that was when it was written in the Vedas. They predicted it far before then but it was recorded in writing at that time. And when Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu appeared, the Lord himself Sri Krishna - he came to deliver what no other incarnation had ever delivered before:

namo mahā-vadānyāya

kṛṣṇa-prema-pradāya te

kṛṣṇāya kṛṣṇa-caitanya-

nāmne gaura-tviṣe namaḥ

[CC Madhya 19.53]

[O most munificent incarnation! You are Kṛṣṇa Himself appearing as Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya Mahāprabhu. You have assumed the golden color of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī, and You are widely distributing pure love of Kṛṣṇa. We offer our respectful obeisances unto You.]

He came to deliver the ultimate goal of yoga - love of God freely for everyone. For no cost other than your faith. And he personally demonstrated by his own example this process of chanting the holy names - the spiritual sound vibration of the absolute truth and dancing very gracefully for the pleasure of God. There was a class of very very learned philosophers who thought what is this nonsense dancing and chanting. It is the process for sentimentalists. When Caitanya Mahaprabhu came to Varanasi, the most learned scholars and most renowned sannyasis on earth headed by Prakasananda sarasvati they had nothing but criticism and accusations against Caitanya Mahaprabhu. “That we are studying the Shastra in learned and quiet circles and we are performing tapasya and meditation, this is the process of purifying the heart. This Caitanya, he has become like a madman. What is this? This process of chanting and dancing is meant for women and children - the less intelligent. We hear that wherever he goes hundreds and thousands of people become his followers and begin to dance and chant with him. This is because in kali yuga people are sentimental, they're less intelligent and they're just outright fallen. But if he thinks that he's going to influence us with his sentimentalism he is in for a surprise. He will not get very far in this high seat of spiritual learning of Varanasi.”

So Caitanya Mahaprabhu, what could he say to such persons. Unless you open your heart to hear someone... even God will not... he cannot convince you. In Bhagavad-gita, as long as Arjuna considered that ‘I know better than you Krishna,’ Krishna had not a single word to say to him. He was simply acting as his chariot driver.. han.. “Okay Arjuna where do you want to go. I have nothing to say except where do you want to go.” He had nothing to say to him. Arjuna was speaking all learned philosophy according to his calculation and Krishna simply remained silent waiting for Arjuna to open his heart. And when Arjuna finally humbled himself and said Krishna I am a fool I know nothing.

kārpaṇya-doṣopahata-svabhāvaḥ
pṛcchāmi tvāṁ dharma-sammūḍha-cetāḥ
yac chreyaḥ syān niścitaṁ brūhi tan me
śiṣyas te ’haṁ śādhi māṁ tvāṁ prapannam

[Bg 2.7]

[Now I am confused about my duty and have lost all composure because of miserly weakness. In this condition I am asking You to tell me for certain what is best for me. Now I am Your disciple, and a soul surrendered unto You. Please instruct me.]

‘Krishna, I am confused, bewildered, I am a fool please instruct me. I am your disciple. You are my only hope.. Save me …please.’ It wasn't until Arjuna confessed that he was a fool that Krishna considered him worthy of speaking to him. And it was at that time that Krishna spoke Bhagavad-gita. We should all know that we are sitting in the same chariot as Arjuna and Krishna has nothing to say to you until you humble yourself, open your heart and say, “Krishna, I'm a fool what should I do.” Han… until you honestly and genuinely approach Krishna like that you are not qualified to understand his message. And we see so many learned scholars they study the scriptures but they never understand the essence.

Lord Chaitanya transforms the hearts of the Mayavadis

So Caitanya Mahaprabhu, although so many people repeatedly requested Lord Caitanya, “Why don't you defeat those crazy mayavadis? Do you know what they're saying about you? They're blaspheming you. It breaks our heart to hear this.” Caitanya Mahaprabhu…he had nothing to say to them. But then one day he was invited to take lunch at the same time that they were. So he purposely came a little late. They were all seated. Prakasananda and many many many of these great stalwarts, scholars, sannyasis. And when Lord Caitanya entered the assembly, the first thing he did is he began to radiate the effulgence of the brahmajyoti from his body and of course as impersonalists their goal of life is to merge into this Brahma Jyoti. So they were very attracted by the inconceivable beauty of the Lord. And then the Lord, although he was manifesting such infinite beauty, he accepted the most humble position. They were all seated according to their high positions in very high seats. Caitanya Mahaprabhu at the place where everyone washed their feet before coming in the door he sat there. And the leader of the Mayavadis, he said haan… we can all see that you are Brahman manifested on this earth. You are very great, very beautiful why you are sitting in this lowly place. Lord Caitanya said no no no you are all very great learned scholars. I am just a very simple sentimental sannyasi. I have no right to sit on such a high level with all of you. So Prakasananda took him by the hand and brought him to sit amongst them and because he rendered that little service, he opened his heart and thus he prepared the way for his own salvation. At that time, they inquired from Lord Caitanya, “Why, you’re a sannyasi why aren't you with us studying Shastra; doing Tapasya… this is the man's work. Why you're doing the work of women and children-dancing and chanting.”

Lord Caitanya spoke and his message is of utmost importance for everyone who aspires for God consciousness to understand. Caitanya Mahaprabhu said that, “When I approached my GuruMmaharaj he told me, “Son, you are a fool. You should just chant Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare, Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. You'll never understand all this philosophy. Just chant Hare Krishna.” So I took these words of my Guru as my life and soul and I began to chant Hare Krishna and tears started flowing from my eyes and my hair started standing on end and I just wanted to dance and I went back to my guru and I said what's happening what's going on. I am dancing, I'm crying… what is this chanting doing to me. And my guru said..ah you have attained the supreme perfection of life. In Kali Yuga that's all that is required. Just chant Hare Krishna.”

harer nāma harer nāma

harer nāmaiva kevalam

kalau nāsty eva nāsty eva

nāsty eva gatir anyathā

[CC Madhya 6.242]

[In this age of quarrel and hypocrisy, the only means of deliverance is the chanting of the holy names of the Lord. There is no other way. There is no other way. There is no other way.]

‘In the age of Kali Yuga according to the Shastra there is no other way of salvation except the chanting of the Holy Name.’

Then Caitanya Mahaprabhu went on according to logic, reason, philosophy and strong scriptural evidence he totally defeated every philosophical argument of these mayavadi sannyasis. They were absolutely amazed how can anyone possibly possess such philosophical wisdom and such realization of the Sastra. They all looked and felt like complete fools before him and then they began to consider - if such a supremely learned scholar as him is too foolish to understand the Vedas but just chants Hare Krishna then what is our position. No wonder we are so fed up with all of our studies and then they humbled themselves before Lord Caitanya and Lord Caitanya turned them and trained them how to chant the Hare Krishna mantra and they all took to the process of Harinaam Sankirtan as their life and soul. So do not think that this chanting and dancing is simply some sentimental process. But the greatest, most stalwart, most learned philosophers, logicians and authorities of the scripture… they accept that this is the conclusion of all philosophy, of all knowledge, to glorify God by chanting his name.

Essence of all scriptures

I was recently speaking with a very very learned Sadhu. He's about seventy-eight years old. He's been studying the scripture and he's from at the age of eight he left his home and became a Sadhu and for seventy years he was traveling around practicing different yogic systems, studying different paths, studying the scriptures and he told me that if you read every scripture scrutinizingly you'll find that there's only one conclusion - Hari Naam, to chant the holy name. In the age of Kali Yuga this is the conclusion and Srila Prabhupada, our spiritual master he wrote over sixty books. Perhaps the most clear and precise, highly philosophical books ever written in English. They are original translations of the most essential Vedic scriptures and repeatedly he explained that the purpose of all our philosophy, the purpose of all our preaching is only for one purpose. We are not concerned with defeating people. We are not concerned with conquering opposing philosophies. That is not our goal. Our goal is one - to induce people to chant the holy names of God. That is the conclusion of all philosophy because it is the only process in Kali yuga recommended by which we can attain the supreme destination of love of God, Prema Bhakti.

Other processes of yoga can certainly bring us peace of mind, can certainly provide us with great mystic siddhis, even bring us to that point of Mukti or liberation but prema pum-artho mahan . The supreme destination of pure love of God can only be attained through bhakti and bhakti is practiced in Kali Yuga by the chanting of God's name as the basic foundation of our life. When we build a foundation of our spiritual life on the Holy Name of God then whatever we do within this world whether we are kings, Chartered Accountants, sadhus, businessman, lawyers, agriculturalists, students, housewives. We will have the proper understanding and realization by which we can dedicate our activities in the service of God and attain the supreme perfection of liberation.

These are the words spoken by the nine Yogendras to Maharaj Nimi and in these words which have been immortalized in the hearts of great Saints throughout the generations, we are given something very practical, very tangible by which we can take shelter of and make spiritual progress and attain the real interest of human life. By taking shelter of the holy names and by taking shelter of those great souls who are genuinely and honestly propagating the holy names.. That is the sure means of success in this age of Kali Yuga. So let us chant Hare Krishna and be happy. Let us understand that this process of chanting and dancing is not simply some funfair…han.. Some carnival dance where everyone is simply enjoying. Yes.. Devotees are enjoying, they are enjoying immensely more than any materialist can understand because the enjoyment is coming from the purity of their hearts, it is coming from a reciprocation of God but this is the most authorized scientific process of yoga which is meant for the ultimate purification of the mind, the heart and the soul.

Thank you very much. Hare Krishna. Is there any questions? Does anyone have any questions?

Q & A Transcription

Devotee: 42:55 to 43:32 [ Not very clear, the devotee is asking about what is the purpose of creation.]

Maharaj: The purpose of creation is to facilitate the desires of all living beings. You see the spiritual world, the eternal abode of God, param dhaam, is a place for those healthy devoted souls who simply want to live according to their natural position in devotion to Lord Sri Krishna… Lord Hari. But each living entity is qualitatively one with God and therefore each living entity, each soul having the qualities of God have the quality of independence. Swarat is one of the divine qualities of Krishna. So, each of his expansions also has that quality of Swarat or independence and Krishna never interferes or takes away that independence. So those who do not want to live for the pleasure of God but those who want to take the position of God, although they cannot but they want to try, they want to be the enjoyer rather than be the enjoyed, for such souls there's absolutely no place for them in the spiritual world because in the spiritual world everyone is simply living for the pleasure of Krishna and to have such souls in the spiritual world would only cause a disturbance.

Therefore the Lord has to have a place for them. Therefore he creates this material existence specifically and exclusively for such persons who want to enjoy separate from God and the proof is that if you look, of the eight four lakh species of life everyone in the material world is trying to enjoy, enjoy separate from God. Han.. therefore it is a place to facilitate their separate interest but out of compassion he designs it in such a way that there's so much suffering and misery essentially due to its temporary nature that eventually it will cure the disease of the desire to enjoy separate from God and people will eventually come to the point that my real natural position is to surrender to God and when we by our own free will decide to surrender to God then we are again promoted to our natural condition in the spiritual world. This material world in Sanskrit is called Durga. Durga literally means prison. This creation is a prison house for those who have misused their independence and act sinfully against the laws of God against the will of God. Why in a state in every country do we find that the government builds a prison, [Audio break-47.18] world where there is not a prison, because there will be good citizens and there will be bad citizens. It is inevitable. Some people will be law-abiding and act according to the laws of the state but it is inevitable that some will live against the laws of the state. So there must be a place to rectify their behaviour.

In America, the prison houses are called correctional institutions. That is the legal formal name. They're not called prisons… correctional institutions.. that means they are Institutes created by the government to correct the misbehaviour and make people into good citizens again. So this material creation is the Lord's correctional Institution and that is why it was created. It is a facility to teach us the real life of happiness and bliss in the service of God and the Lord himself incarnates:

yadā yadā hi dharmasya
glānir bhavati bhārata
abhyutthānam adharmasya
tadātmānaṁ sṛjāmy aham

[Bg 4.7]

[Whenever and wherever there is a decline in religious practice, O descendant of Bharata, and a predominant rise of irreligion – at that time I descend Myself.]

He comes again and again and again in every millennium to this material creation just to personally remind us of the real goal of life and he sends the Gurus, the Sadhus, the prophets and the great Saints to teach us the process of correcting our life in coming back to our natural spiritual condition and Krishna says in Gita:

janma karma ca me divyam

evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ

tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma

naiti mām eti so ’rjuna

[BG 4.9]

[One who knows the transcendental nature of My appearance and activities does not, upon leaving the body, take his birth again in this material world, but attains My eternal abode, O Arjuna.]

When Krishna appears he reveals his true glory, to attract our minds and the great saints they are constantly instructing us in the glories of the Lord to attract our minds back to him and Krishna says for one who understands the transcendental nature of my appearance and activities in this world such a person never takes birth again in this prison house of material existence but he returns to his supreme abode.

So, the question that we must ask is are we taking advantage of the facility that Krishna is giving us. If you are in a prison house your goal should be to get out not to make life better in the prison house.. Yes of course you should work to make life go better in the prison house but that's not your ultimate goal. That's just a temporary matter that has to be dealt with but the ultimate goal is to get out, to become free. Only a fool in the prison house makes his ultimate goal to have a nice carpet, to get a refrigerator, to get a television set, to get promoted to a better prison cell.han.. That is foolishness. Why waste your time with these things when that same energy could be utilized to get out. But the foolish creatures in this Kali Yuga, they like the prison house very much and they're spending their whole lives to try to make a permanent place of this prison house, they want to live as long as they possibly can and get as many nice items to put in their cell as possible and they don't even consider the hope of getting out.

But Krishna says:

yaṁ yaṁ vāpi smaran bhāvaṁ

tyajaty ante kalevaram

taṁ tam evaiti kaunteya

sadā tad-bhāva-bhāvitaḥ

[Bg 8.6]

[Whatever state of being one remembers when he quits his body, O son of Kuntī, that state he will attain without fail.]

All you have to do is remember me at the time of death and you'll get out. If you simply remember Krishna at that most essential time, you will be freed from this prison house forever and return to your eternal natural position. So therefore this life is meant to practice remembering him. Factually we can find the kingdom of God, we can be released from this prison even in this very life, by finding the spiritual abode within our own hearts and the most sublime and simplest process of remembering Krishna is to hear about him, to associate with his devotees and to chant his holy name. The real purpose of the creation is to bring us to that point of going through enough frustrations and suffering through the repetition of birth and death where we take shelter of the Lord's name and the Lord's devotees and we are escorted by their mercy back to the spiritual world..

Is there any other questions?

Devotee: [Unclear -53:18]

Maharaj: Krishna and Rama are the same person. The Supreme Absolute truth, the Personality of Godhead as described in Bhagavatam and described in Bhagavad-gita appears in various forms in this material world for the purpose of fulfilling specific purposes. It is described in Bhagavatam –

ete cāṁśa-kalāḥ puṁsaḥ kṛṣṇas tu bhagavān svayam

indrāri-vyākulaṁ lokaṁ mṛḍayanti yuge yuge

[SB 1.3.28]

[All of the above-mentioned incarnations are either plenary portions or portions of the plenary portions of the Lord, but Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa is the original Personality of Godhead. All of them appear on planets whenever there is a disturbance created by the atheists. The Lord incarnates to protect the theists.]

That Krishna appears in many ways. The original form of Godhead is declared to be Krishna. From Krishna, he expands himself as Balarama who expands himself as Narayan, who expands himself as Pradyumna, Anirrudha, Sankarshana, and Vasudev. They expand themselves as Mahavishnu, Karanodakshayi Vishnu. He expands himself as Garbodakshayi Vishnu, the lotus flower stems from the navel of Garbodakshayi Vishnu and Brahma is born of that and he appears within each universe and then Ksirodakshayi Vishnu is born… not born but expanding from him and he appears within the heart of every living being.

So in this way all of these Vishnu expansions are the one same supreme person Krishna. There is no difference. They are simply… Krishna is inconceivable and almighty. Bhagavan can expand himself in innumerable forms, each one self-same exactly identical in personality to his original person in order to fulfil different particular functions. So it is explained when there is a time of distress within this material world in order to -

paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ

vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām

dharma-saṁsthāpanārthāya

sambhavāmi yuge yuge

[Bg 4.8]

[To deliver the pious and to annihilate the miscreants, as well as to reestablish the principles of religion, I Myself appear, millennium after millennium.]

In order to establish true religious principles, annihilate the demons and protect the pious the Lord appears. So the Lord appeared in this divine form of Lord Ramachandra. He appeared as a Kshatriya and he killed the demon Ravana. So what is the difference between Rama and Krishna? The answer is there is no difference. It is this he is the same personality but that divine Krishna of Vraja who enjoys playing with the cows in his eternal abode of Goloka, he appeared in the form of a great warrior in order to kill the demon Ravana and to re-establish the principles of religion during the Tretha Yuga. So the worship of Rama and the worship of Krishna is the worship of the one same supreme person and those who worship Ram will go to the spiritual world and they will eternally associate with that divine form of Ram in Vaikunta and now those who worship Krishna will attain the spiritual world and God will reveal himself and in the divine form of Govinda in Goloka Vrindavan. Same person but he reveals himself according to how you love him, according to the form you worship and love he will reveal himself to you accordingly.

Is there any other questions?

Devotee: [Unclear 58:14]

Maharaj: It isn't …often times that is the case. Krishna says in Gita –

bhogaiśvarya-prasaktānāṁ
tayāpahṛta-cetasām
vyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ
samādhau na vidhīyate

[Bg 2.44]

[In the minds of those who are too attached to sense enjoyment and material opulence, and who are bewildered by such things, the resolute determination for devotional service to the Supreme Lord does not take place.]

‘For those who are too much attached to material opulence and sense enjoyment, the resolute determination for devotion to God cannot take place.’ There are two ways of learning - one is by hearing and one is by experience. Now you can factually become exhausted by either of these two processes..han.. Experience is the best teacher and a fool learns by no other way. The process of suffering and attaining experience through suffering and learning is the process for fools. But let's face it most of us are fools…haan. An intelligent person does not have to go through the experience of suffering; he hears and with good intelligence accepts those words. Krishna descends in this world, he sends the sadhus, he sends the saints to tell us –

mām upetya punar janma
duḥkhālayam aśāśvatam
nāpnuvanti mahātmānaḥ
saṁsiddhiṁ paramāṁ gatāḥ

[Bg 8.15]

[After attaining Me, the great souls, who are yogīs in devotion, never return to this temporary world, which is full of miseries, because they have attained the highest perfection.]

That those great souls, the Mahatmas they make their only goal the spiritual world. They are not content in this world which is full of miseries due to its temporary nature. This world is temporary and therefore it is destined to create miseries upon our life. We are eternal; our satisfaction must be on the eternal platform. When the eternal soul seeks satisfaction on the temporary realm of material existence he is destined to be frustrated and then suffer. Anyone could see through their ears by hearing that the more you become attached to something in this world the more you must suffer when it's taken away and it will be taken away. The more we become attached to the senses the more agitated and disturbed our mind becomes because our senses are never satisfied..han... Now the question is are we willing to accept the Word of God, are we willing to accept the words of the sadhus and the words of the scriptures. Then factually there is no need to be smashed and materially exhausted. You can through, through the process of good intelligence, you take to the spiritual process and you are materially exhausted even in the position you are now. You understand... you understand material exhaustion without having to go through it. That is an intelligent person. If you are not willing to accept through the process of hearing then you have to suffer, you have to be kicked by Maya repeatedly birth after birth after birth until finally you're so materially exhausted that you cry out Krishna save me. Eventually you have to come to one of those two conclusions in your material journey…han..

So why not just accept these words tonight, right now and take to the spiritual path otherwise the only other alternative is you go on trying to enjoy until finally inevitably you become completely exhausted and suffering and then you say Krishna I've had enough save me…han.. Do we have to go through all of that? Haven't we experienced enough suffering in what we've seen in what we've gone through our in this life. It's enough of an indication that yes what I really want the real goal is self-realization. So yes we have to become materially exhausted but you can become materially exhausted by hearing without having to go through the suffering or you have or you will be materially exhausted through repeated thrashings by material nature..han.. Some of us may be thinking what is this material thrashings I'm very rich, I have nice children, I'm healthy and everything is just the way I like it in this world.. han…such a person is most unfortunate. A person who is suffering is more fortunate because at least he understands the reality of his existence. Such a person is so intoxicated he doesn't understand what is right before him… at any moment as we say in English - the rug will be pulled out from under your feet and you'll fall and break your head…han.. in the form of time… Krishna does not allow anyone to remain content, in the form of time he will take away your loved ones, he will take away your health, he will take away everything and at the time of death you'll simply be looking around thinking ‘My God I have wasted my life, what do I have, what is left.’

Is there any other questions?

Thank you very much for coming and joining us this evening. We are very grateful for Mahaprabhu for so graciously and mercifully arranging this wonderful facility for us to come and chant the holy names together, dance together and hear the philosophy of the goal of life. This really is the highest duty of those in the Grihasta Ashram- to utilize their resources to facilitate one's friends, one's family members and as many others as possible to understand the real goal of life, to associate with devotees, to chant the names of God and to develop a real love and unity amongst each other in God's service. So we are very thankful for Mahaprabhu, for taking this trouble which is certainly a labor of love for the benefit of all of us and we very highly recommend that everyone regularly conducts such meetings either on a small level or a large level whatever is one’s capacity, in one's own home or in some place where devotees can conveniently come together, people in general, friends and family members can really enrich their consciousness by meditating upon and pondering these, the greatest of all subjects, the goal of life - Krishna consciousness. So we'd like to thank everyone for…

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
